concepts.name: Medium of Exchange
concepts.brief_description: A medium used to exchange goods and services instead of conflict or barter.

concepts.name: Government Account at Central Bank
concepts.brief_description: An account where the government holds funds that exist at the central bank.

concepts.name: Credit/Crediting
concepts.brief_description: The process of adding money to an account, similar to depositing cash or transferring funds, often referred to as 'credit' in financial contexts.

concepts.name: Physical Notes
concepts.brief_description: Money created by physical printing on paper bills by the central bank.

concepts.name: Banks as Pipelines
concepts.brief_description: Banks act as intermediaries where people deposit money and banks lend out a portion of it, which is then redeposited by borrowers, creating an ongoing circulation mechanism.

concepts.name: Inflation
concepts.brief_description: The loss of value in currency over time due to an increase in supply relative to the demand for goods and services.

concepts.name: Balanced Inflation
concepts.brief_description: A moderate level of inflation that keeps the economy moving smoothly, preventing deflation or hyperinflation.

concepts.name: Investment as a Defense against Inflation
concepts.brief_description: The strategy of investing long-term money into assets that can beat inflation to protect purchasing power and ensure growth over time. This may include buying real estate, stocks, bonds, etc., rather than keeping cash in savings accounts or low-interest investment options like fixed deposits or mutual funds.
